outputs.name: search_results
outputs.guarantees: sorted by score descending, max 50 results

operations.name: execute_search
operations.description: Perform full-text search across indexed documents
preconditions: index has been built at least once
postconditions: results contain only documents matching query terms
results are sorted by relevance score descending
each result has a valid snippet with highlighted terms

edge_cases.narrative: the operator searches for something that does not exist yet

edge_cases.expected_behavior: return informative empty state, prompt to run indexer
edge_cases.narrative: the operator has just set up the system

error_states.behavior: return cached results if available, otherwise graceful error with retry suggestion

invariants: search results never include documents the operator does not have access to
search index is eventually consistent with source data (max 60s lag)
no search query mutates stored data

constitution_refs.constraint: all state in PostgreSQL, no file-based state
all errors return structured ErrorResult, never raw exceptions
all endpoints require authentication, no anonymous access
p95 response time under 200ms for read operations

task_map.title: implement full-text search index builder
task_map.description: create the indexing pipeline that processes source documents

acceptance_criteria.criterion: index builder processes all files in configured directories
index is queryable within 60s of source file change

task_map.notes: use existing pg_trgm extension for fuzzy matching

task_map.title: implement search query executor
task_map.description: the core search operation that queries the index

acceptance_criteria.criterion: search returns results sorted by relevance score
empty query returns informative empty state

task_map.title: wire search module into application entry point
task_map.description: graft the search module's exports into the CLI dispatcher

acceptance_criteria.criterion: no ImportError when loading the modified entry point

task_map.notes: graft-only task — no standalone module produced

exports.name: capture_usage
exports.description: Extract token usage from pydantic-ai agent result

exports.name: build_usage_limits
exports.signature: def build_usage_limits(budget: int, *, reserve_pct: float = 0.1) -> UsageLimits
exports.description: Construct pydantic-ai UsageLimits from a token budget integer

exports.name: classify_limit_exceeded
exports.description: Classify a UsageLimitExceeded exception into an error category
preconditions: exc is an instance of UsageLimitExceeded
postconditions: return value is always one of the three known categories

host_imports_required: from token_budget import capture_usage, build_usage_limits, classify_limit_exceeded, TokenUsage

graft_points.anchor_text: from factory_types import AgentRunError, Err, HandoffArtifact, Ok, Result
graft_points.content: from token_budget import capture_usage, build_usage_limits, classify_limit_exceeded
graft_points.description: Import the new module's exports into the coding agent

graft_points.content:
usage = capture_usage(agent_result)
d = _ok_dict(start_ms, trace_path, git_sha, tokens_input=usage.input_tokens, tokens_output=usage.output_tokens)
graft_points.description: Wire token capture into the success path after agent.run_sync completes
Register the module in the agent registry for discoverability

called_by.context: after agent.run_sync completes, captures token usage from the result
builds UsageLimits before agent invocation to enforce token budget

calls.context: reading input token count from agent result usage data
reading output token count from agent result usage data
constructing limits object to pass to agent.run_sync

call_graph.notes: This module is a leaf — it depends on pydantic-ai but nothing in the host codebase depends on it until grafted.
